data: News, announcements, and updates from the Clef language project.
Welcome to the Clef language documentation. Here you’ll find design rationale, tutorials, reference material, and compiler internals.

Design documents provide the informative companion layer to the formal specification. These articles explain the why behind language decisions — the research, trade-offs, and motivations that shaped Clef.
Many of these documents were originally published on the SpeakEZ Technologies blog during the early design phase of the Fidelity Framework and have been updated to reflect current naming and project structure.
Practical guides for getting started with Clef, from installation through building your first project.
Clef is under active development. This guide will be expanded as the toolchain matures.
data.ecosystem#Ecosystem: Tool Purpose CLI Composer Compiler and build orchestration composer ClefPak Source-based package manager clefpak / cpk Lattice Language server (LSP) integrated Atelier IDE extensions (VS Code, Vim) —
data.overview#Overview:
Clef is a systems programming language that combines:
Algebraic effects for composable side-effect management Dependent types for compile-time correctness guarantees Proof-aware compilation for verified software at native performance

data: Architecture documentation for the Composer compiler, Alex middle-end, Lattice compiler services, and related tooling.
Reference documentation for the Clef ecosystem: Alloy standard libraries, Composer CLI, and ClefPak package manager.

The Clef Language Specification defines the syntax and semantics of the Clef programming language.
The specification is maintained in the clef-lang-spec repository. Use the “Edit this page” and “Discuss this section” links on any spec page to contribute.
data.how-to-read-this-specification#How to Re:
The specification is the normative reference for the Clef language. It uses precise language (SHALL, MUST, MAY) to define required behavior.
For explanatory context and design rationale, see the Design Rationale section, which provides the informative companion layer explaining why the language is designed as it is.
